title: (No AI) The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A photo of Sam Altman](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2236544097.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples. That's according to [a blockbuster lawsuit](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28453238-complaint-for-trade-secret-misappropriation-and-breach-of-contract/) filed by Apple, which accuses [OpenAI of stealing confidential documents](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964350/apple-openai-lawsuit-trade-secrets), spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of its trusted partners into performing a proprietary product design technique.

The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:

*   Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964843/apple-openai-lawsuit-wildest-claims)
contentSnippet:
When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples. That's according to a blockbuster lawsuit filed by Apple, which accuses OpenAI of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of its trusted partners into performing a proprietary product design technique.
The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:
Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

Read the full story at The Verge.

That's according to a blockbuster lawsuit filed by Apple, which accuses OpenAI of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of […]

title: (No AI) Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![An image showing Waze’s conversational reporting feature](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25708121/waze_conversational_report.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Waze is getting an AI makeover. Google is integrating its flagship AI assistant, Gemini, into the driving app with the goal of letting users personalize their trips a little more.

Of the four new updates, only two are being described as involving Gemini. Waze says its updating its conversation reporting feature, [first introduced in 2024](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/31/24284037/waze-report-incidents-voice), to allow drivers to use conversational voice commands to report traffic incidents and suggest map updates, like a road closure or outdated house number. In addition, Waze introduced Destination Search, enabling drivers to use (again) use conversation voice commands (e.g., "Find me a coffee shop that's open …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/964132/waze-gemini-ai-voice-commands-less-chatty)

title: (No AI) Scientists’ Side Hustle? Using AI and Quantum Computing to Generate New Peptides
